title_header: Omniscient Reader, Episode 5: The Beginning of the Paywall Part 3

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: THIS EPISODE CONTAINS DEPICTIONS OF VIOLENCE THAT MAY BE UPSETTING FOR SOME READERS.

dialogue.speaker: Kim Dokja
dialogue.line: F**k! What is this?! What the hell is going on?!

dialogue.speaker: Kim Dokja
dialogue.line: That is strange. Why hasn't he appeared yet?

dialogue.speaker: Kim Dokja
dialogue.line: The events I'm familiar with... and those I didn't know were even part of the story, were all happening at once. If I could just read TWSA again, I could know what to do, but...

dialogue.speaker: Kim Dokja
dialogue.line: No. Damn it! I guess it was removed. But why? This series isn't even popular, so I doubt someone would've tried to illegally share it.

dialogue.speaker: Kim Dokja
dialogue.line: Wait a minute.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: MR. DOKJA KIM, STARTING AT 7 PM KST, CONTENT WILL TODAY BECOME PAID. THIS WILL HELP - WISH YOU ALL THE BEST.

dialogue.speaker: Kim Dokja
dialogue.line: No... It can't be...

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: [1 ATTACHED FILE] SAVE ALL THREE WAYS TO SURVIVE THE APOCALYPSE.TXT

dialogue.speaker: Kim Dokja
dialogue.line: Did he send me his novel as a present?! Isn't this illegal sharing? Doesn't matter since he's the author?

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: YOU HAVE RECEIVED AN EXCLUSIVE ATTRIBUTE. A SKILL HAS BEEN UNLOCKED.

dialogue.speaker: Kim Dokja
dialogue.line: Each of the TWSA survivors receive a special attribute, and a skill that is linked to that attribute. It is just like a game.

dialogue.speaker: Kim Dokja
dialogue.line: Then I'll need to figure out my attribute.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: [ATTRIBUTES WINDOW CANNOT BE OPENED]

dialogue.speaker: Kim Dokja
dialogue.line: What? What the hell is going on? If I can't access my attribute window, I won't be able to find out what my attribute and skill are!

dialogue.speaker: Kim Dokja
dialogue.line: Oh, well. I'll just read the attached file first.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: [YOUR ATTRIBUTE HAS BEEN ACTIVATED: YOU ARE NOW ABLE TO READ AT A FASTER SPEED]

dialogue.speaker: Kim Dokja
dialogue.line: W-What? That was my attribute? It just took me less than a minute to read the opening chapter of TWSA. But thanks to that...

dialogue.speaker: Kim Dokja
dialogue.line: Bingo!

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: He looked at the people gathered at the subway cabin number 3707. He felt the coldness of the space. He promised himself not to make a mistake. He will do whatever it takes to achieve it. People's lives were frozen in fear. He felt no remorse.

dialogue.speaker: Kim Dokja
dialogue.line: It was cabin number 3707. With that, I realized why I had felt so uneasy: The reason the events unfolding around me were different from what I remembered was that the protagonist of TWSA's prologue was actually in the subway cabin behind this one.

dialogue.speaker: Kim Dokja
dialogue.line: Wait, then... What happened to the people in 3807 in the story?

dialogue.speaker: Yoo Sangah
dialogue.line: Dokja. Shouldn't we try to stop them?

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: "He took a peek at the chaos and confusion that was unfolding in subway cabin 3807." "It is probably too late for them. After all, only two people will survive in that cabin."

dialogue.speaker: Kim Namwoon
dialogue.line: F**k! Where the hell do you think you're going?

dialogue.speaker: Kim Namwoon
dialogue.line: Shut your mouth!

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Please... L-Let me go...!

dialogue.speaker: Kim Dokja
dialogue.line: I knew who those two people were.

dialogue.speaker: Kim Namwoon
dialogue.line: Didn't I tell you to shut up?

dialogue.speaker: Kim Dokja
dialogue.line: Sangah will probably die. Everyone in this cabin will die, except for two. Including me.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Han
dialogue.line: What do you think you're doing? You little brat! Didn't they teach you to respect your elders...?

dialogue.speaker: Kim Dokja
dialogue.line: Mr. Han?!

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Han
dialogue.line: Huh?

dialogue.speaker: Kim Namwoon
dialogue.line: What the hell, old man? Do you wanna die too? What? Can't you see what is going on here?

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Han
dialogue.line: What the hell are you talking about, you brat?!

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Han
dialogue.line: Over there. Don't you see that?

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: H-Help me. Please!

dialogue.speaker: Kim Namwoon
dialogue.line: Ahh! Die! Die!!

dialogue.speaker: Kim Namwoon
dialogue.line: Don't you get it? The army isn't going to save us. And someone has to die.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: P-Please, spare me! I've been good all my life!

dialogue.speaker: Kim Namwoon
dialogue.line: Shut up and die!

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: W-What are you-?

dialogue.speaker: Kim Namwoon
dialogue.line: We have to pick the ones who will die.

dialogue.speaker: Kim Namwoon
dialogue.line: I know exactly what you're thinking. You think we're above all that. How can we kill others just to survive like we're some kind of animals? Normally, I would agree with you. But what about the situation we're in now? We have no choice. It is kill or be killed. Who can blame us when our own lives are on the line? Think wisely. The world you knew just ended. And in this new world, we have new rules to follow.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: Namwoon Kim: He was one of the earliest adopters to accept the world of TWSA. It was Namwoon Kim who drove a wedge into the crack and destroyed the sense of morality that had been binding society together.

dialogue.speaker: Kim Namwoon
dialogue.line: Are you all just gonna sit and watch? Do you all wanna die? F**k this. To hell with it!

dialogue.speaker: Kim Namwoon
dialogue.line: If no one dies within the next five minutes, everyone in here will die!

dialogue.speaker: Kim Dokja
dialogue.line: I suddenly recalled a line I had read a long time ago. "Executioners pull the lever of the electric chair at the same time." "They do so in order to conceal who really killed the prisoner." It was as if, I was reading that part of the novel. They were in a different world, and I was just a spectator. All I could do was just stand by and watch. That nameless elderly woman became the first victim.

dialogue.speaker: Kim Dokja
dialogue.line: If I carelessly meddle with the course of the story, there may be less of the original plot left for me to work with. But is it fair to stand by and watch her die... just so that I can live?

dialogue.speaker: Yoo Sangah
dialogue.line: They're going to kill her!

dialogue.speaker: Kim Dokja
dialogue.line: Don't be so reckless! It's dangerous!

dialogue.speaker: Yoo Sangah
dialogue.line: I know, but...

dialogue.speaker: Kim Dokja
dialogue.line: If you go, they'll kill you too.

dialogue.speaker: Kim Dokja
dialogue.line: Sangah... I guess some people were just destined to shine, regardless of the genre they are in. But Sangah cannot change the course of the story, because she isn't the heroine in this world.

dialogue.speaker: Kim Dokja
dialogue.line: Sangah, stay here.

dialogue.speaker: Yoo Sangah
dialogue.line: What? But...

dialogue.speaker: Kim Dokja
dialogue.line: Please. Just do as I tell you for now. If you listen to me this once, I promise I won't ever bother you again.

dialogue.speaker: Kim Dokja
dialogue.line: It is too early for me to interfere. This wasn't part of my plan.

dialogue.speaker: Yoo Sangah
dialogue.line: Dokja?

dialogue.speaker: Kim Dokja
dialogue.line: The reason I didn't interfere sooner wasn't because I was afraid of Namwoon or the others, or because I condone their savage ways. I was just waiting... for the right moment. And that moment is... now!!

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: MAIN SCENARIO #1 [PROVE YOUR VALUE] KILL ONE OR MORE LIVING CREATURES. CATEGORY: MAIN DIFFICULTY: F TIME LIMIT: 30 MINUTES REWARD: 300 COINS PENALTY FOR FAILURE: DEATH. TIME REMAINING: 7 MINUTES 32 SECONDS.

summary: Kim Dokja realizes the events unfolding are different from the novel he read, as he is in a different subway car than the protagonist. As chaos erupts and people resort to violence to survive, Dokja struggles with whether to interfere, ultimately deciding to act when a system message announces the first main scenario.
